description: Phase 2.5 Alpha 2 PRDs for the guide scenario engine: MDX-authored guides under docs/guides/** generate TypeScript scenario tests that run on the scenario layer against TestRuntimeProvider, with source-mapped failures and a focused per-PR CI gate. Stories follow the 4-PRD breakdown in `spec/alpha2/prd-alpha2-{01..04}-*.md`; each story has a corresponding US-XXX section in its PRD with full TDD acceptance criteria. Source of truth: `spec/ROADMAP.md` Phase 2.5 and `spec/17-executable-tutorials.md` (§19).

userStories.title: Publish Alpha 2 GuideFrontmatter schema
userStories.description: As a guide author, I can validate my MDX frontmatter against `GuideFrontmatter` and receive a typed remediation when I use a Beta-only key. (PRD-A2-01 US-057)
acceptanceCriteria: `GuideFrontmatter` Effect Schema lives in `sdk/src/docs/guide-frontmatter.ts` and is re-exported by `@lando/sdk/docs/components` and `@lando/core/schema`

Accepted keys: `id` (kebab-case, required), `defaultLayer` (only `"scenario"`; `"e2e"` schema-rejected with Beta remediation citing §19.11), `provider` (only `"test"`), `timeout` (positive int ms, default 60000), `platforms`, `tags`, `skip` (`{ reason, until? }`), and `deprecated` (schema-only, no runtime propagation in Alpha 2)
Schema-rejected keys with tagged `NotImplementedError` remediation citing §19.16 and Phase 3 Beta: `tabs`, `axes`, `variants`
Schema round-trips through the §13.2 schema snapshot gate without manual fixture edits beyond the snapshot diff
Tests cover happy-path acceptance, each Beta-only key rejection (text assertion), and the `id` kebab-case rule
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Publish Alpha 2 component prop schemas
userStories.description: As a guide author, every Alpha 2 component has a published prop schema that round-trips through encode/decode, with a `MatcherSchema` subset for `<Verify>`. (PRD-A2-01 US-058)
acceptanceCriteria: Prop schemas exist for `<Guide>`, `<Scenario>`, `<Step>`, `<Run>`, `<Verify>`, `<Cleanup>`, `<Variable>`, `<Hidden>`, `<UseFixture>` in `sdk/src/docs/components/*.ts`, re-exported from `@lando/sdk/docs/components` and `@lando/core/docs/components`
`ScenarioProps`: `id` required, `render` defaults to `true`, `reason` required when `render === false` (≥ 8 chars), `tags`, `layer` accepts `"scenario"` only (`"e2e"` rejected with Beta remediation)
`RunProps` accepts exactly one of `command` or `shell`; `runtime` and `tooling` schema-rejected with Beta remediation

`VerifyProps` accepts exactly one of `event`, `command`, `file`, `errorTag`; `tooling`/`runtime` rejected. `expect` validates against the Alpha 2 `MatcherSchema` subset (scalar deep-equal, partial-object, `regex`, `schema`, `anyOf`, `not`); `exact`/`allOf`/`oneOf` are Beta+
`HiddenProps` parses but every generator path emits `NotImplementedError(commandId: "guide.component.hidden", specSection: "§19.3")` pointing at `<Scenario render={false}>`
`VariableProps` requires `name` and `value`, `display` optional; `UseFixtureProps` requires `name`
All schemas round-trip through the §13.2 schema gate
Tests cover each schema's accept set and at least one rejection path with remediation text assertion
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Ship ScenarioContext and Alpha 2 testing helpers
userStories.description: As a generated scenario, I can import `withScenarioContext` from `@lando/core/testing` and receive a stable `ScenarioContext` with the Alpha 2 field set. (PRD-A2-01 US-059)
acceptanceCriteria: `ScenarioContext` interface + Effect tag in `core/src/testing/scenario-context.ts`, re-exported from `@lando/core/testing`

Alpha 2 fields: `guideId`, `scenarioId`, `testDir` (under `os.tmpdir()`, removed at finalize unless `KEEP_SCENARIO_DIRS=1`), `runtime` (TestRuntime from Alpha PRD-06), `vars` map, `runCli`, `events`, `transcript` (write-only stub), `fixtures`
Forward-compat fields throw `NotImplementedError` at use: `variant` (returns `{}` always), `shell` runner
`withScenarioContext({ guideId, scenarioId }, body)` is a scoped Effect that creates `testDir`, builds the context, runs `body`, and runs all `Cleanup` finalizers before tearing down
`runCli` uses the Alpha PRD-06 in-process runner (not a subprocess); captured `stdout`, `stderr`, `exitCode`, and event stream are recorded in the transcript stub
Tests cover `withScenarioContext` end-to-end with a fake `Run`; verify `testDir` is removed on success and preserved when `KEEP_SCENARIO_DIRS=1` is set
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Codify fixture-copy discipline
userStories.description: As a guide author using `<UseFixture name="…">`, the named fixture directory is copied into `ScenarioContext.testDir` before mutation and is immutable in its source location. (PRD-A2-01 US-060)
acceptanceCriteria: `ScenarioContext.fixtures.use(name)` resolves to `<testDir>/<name>` after deep-copying from `docs/guides/<guideId>/fixtures/<name>` or `docs/guides/fixtures/<name>` (per-guide overrides shared)
Copy happens before first mutation; mutating the copy does not mutate the source
Missing fixture surfaces tagged `GuideFixtureNotFoundError` listing the resolved candidate paths
Second `<UseFixture>` call for same name in same scenario is a no-op (not a re-copy, not a silent overwrite)
Symbolic links inside fixtures rejected at copy time with tagged `GuideFixtureSymlinkError`
Fixture I/O uses the existing `FileSystem` service, not `node:fs/promises` directly
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Reject Hidden component at SDK boundary with remediation
userStories.description: As a guide author who writes `<Hidden reason="…">` in Alpha 2, I get a typed, source-mapped error directing me at `<Scenario render={false}>` rather than a silent ignore. (PRD-A2-01 US-061)
acceptanceCriteria: `assertAlpha2Component(componentName, hostPath)` helper in `@lando/sdk/docs/components` throws `NotImplementedError(commandId: "guide.component.hidden", specSection: "§19.3")` with the verbatim remediation: "Move this coverage into a colocated `<Scenario render={false}>` per §19.9. `<Hidden>` ships in Phase 3 Beta — see `spec/ROADMAP.md`."
Same helper rejects every non-Alpha-2 component name (`<Inspect>`, `<Tabs>`, `<Tab>`, `<Inline>`, `<Skip>`) with a one-line remediation citing Phase 3 Beta + `spec/ROADMAP.md`
Tests cover each rejected component name and assert exact remediation text
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Implement deterministic MDX walker for build-guide-scenarios
userStories.description: As a maintainer, `scripts/build-guide-scenarios.ts` parses every guide MDX under `docs/guides/**` into a normalized AST that downstream codegen consumes. (PRD-A2-02 US-062)
acceptanceCriteria: Generator discovers `docs/guides/**/*.mdx` and `recipes/*/README.mdx`; missing/empty `docs/guides/` produces zero outputs without error
MDX parsing uses `@mdx-js/mdx` (or chosen Bun-supported equivalent); dep + version recorded in PR description and `package.json#dependencies`
Frontmatter validates against `GuideFrontmatter` from US-057; failure exits 1 with tagged `GuideFrontmatterValidationError` listing field and rejected value
AST walking records `<Guide>` and every immediate `<Scenario>` child; multi-axis (`<Tabs>`/`<Tab>`) rejected with schema-level Beta remediation
Each scenario's component tree flattened into ordered step list: `(stepName, [{ kind: "Run"|"Verify"|"Cleanup"|"Variable"|"UseFixture", props }])`
Three fixture guides under `core/test/codegen/fixtures/guides/`: happy-path single-scenario, multi-scenario with one `render={false}`, Beta-only-component guide (exits 1)
Re-running twice with identical inputs produces byte-identical AST (assert via deterministic JSON representation)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Emit Alpha 2 generated TypeScript from MDX scenarios
userStories.description: As a generated scenario, I am valid TypeScript that imports `withScenarioContext` from `@lando/core/testing`, runs steps in order, and registers a `Cleanup` finalizer. (PRD-A2-02 US-063)
acceptanceCriteria: One file per scenario at `test/scenarios/generated/guides/<guideId>/<scenarioId>.test.ts`
Each file starts with `// @generated`, `// @source: <mdx-path>:<line>`, `// @scenario: <scenarioId>`, and always-empty `// @variant:` placeholder

Generated bodies wrap `withScenarioContext({ guideId, scenarioId }, …)`; each step is a sequential Effect block with its own `// @source` header for that MDX line. `<Variable>` decls land before steps. `<Cleanup>` becomes `Effect.addFinalizer` registered before first step runs

`<Run command="…">` becomes `runCli(command, { answers })` with `<Variable>` interpolation in source order. `<Verify event="…">` becomes an event-stream assertion. `<Verify file="…">` reads under `testDir`. `<Verify errorTag="…">` asserts on captured failure's `_tag`
`<UseFixture name="…">` lands before any mutation as `await fixtures.use(name)`
Variable interpolation `{{name}}` substitutes `value` into generated TS; `display` preserved only as a comment
Generator deterministic: re-run into temp dir and `diff` produces no changes (unit-test asserted)
`tsc --noEmit` over `test/scenarios/generated/guides/**/*.ts` passes
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Generate test-only scenarios without rendering
userStories.description: As a maintainer, a colocated `<Scenario render={false} reason="…">` produces a generated test that runs identically to a reader scenario but is excluded from any future docs render. (PRD-A2-02 US-064)
acceptanceCriteria: `render: false` preserved through AST and emitted into generated file header as `// @render: false`
Generated test runs same `withScenarioContext` body as reader scenarios; only the render bit differs
Missing or `< 8 char` `reason` exits 1 with `GuideHiddenScenarioReasonError` citing §19.9 and PRD-A2-00 hidden-coverage rule
Tests cover: present-reason green path, missing-reason failure, short-reason failure, and a fixture with one reader + one test-only scenario in the same guide (both files generated, both pass)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Wire build-guide-scenarios into bun run codegen and gitignore outputs
userStories.description: As a maintainer, `bun run codegen` invokes `build-guide-scenarios` deterministically, generated outputs are gitignored, and the existing codegen catalog snapshot does not regress. (PRD-A2-02 US-065)
acceptanceCriteria: `scripts/codegen.ts` registers `build-guide-scenarios` as a stage-1 generator before any TypeScript-compiling stage
`.gitignore` ignores `test/scenarios/generated/guides/**`
`bun run codegen` exits 0 on a clean checkout with at least one Alpha 2 fixture guide present
Alpha PRD-07 codegen-drift check covers `build-guide-scenarios` registration in the catalog snapshot
Output directory cleared on each run so deleted scenarios do not leave stale tests
Generator emits guides sorted by relative path and scenarios by lexicographic id (no filesystem-order leakage)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Provide canonical node-postgres Alpha 2 fixture guide
userStories.description: As a maintainer, an end-to-end fixture guide at `docs/guides/node-postgres.mdx` exercises every Alpha 2 component on the scenario layer. (PRD-A2-02 US-066)
acceptanceCriteria: `docs/guides/node-postgres.mdx` uses `<Guide>`, `<Variable>`, `<Scenario render>` (reader), `<Scenario render={false}>` (one hidden case), `<Step>`, `<Run command>`, `<Verify event>` and `<Verify errorTag>`, `<Cleanup>`, and `<UseFixture>`
Reader scenario walks the MVP node-postgres path (`lando init --recipe node-postgres` → `lando start` → `post-start` event verify → `lando destroy -y` cleanup) using `TestRuntimeProvider`
Hidden `<Scenario render={false}>` exercises an invalid-Landofile regression that asserts `LandofileValidationError` with a remediation substring match
Immutable fixture at `docs/guides/node-postgres/fixtures/invalid-service-type/` with deliberately bad `.lando.yml`; hidden scenario calls `<UseFixture name="invalid-service-type" />` before `<Run>`
Both generated tests pass under `bun test test/scenarios/generated/guides/node-postgres/`
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Implement bun run docs:scenario author command
userStories.description: As a guide author, I can run one guide's scenarios from the repo root without re-running the whole `bun test` suite. (PRD-A2-03 US-067)
acceptanceCriteria: `scripts/docs-scenario.ts` wired into `package.json#scripts` as `"docs:scenario": "bun run scripts/docs-scenario.ts"`
`bun run docs:scenario <guideId>` runs `build-guide-scenarios --only <guideId>` then `bun test test/scenarios/generated/guides/<guideId>/`
`--scenario <id>` narrows to one scenario by routing to the matching generated file
`--keep` sets `KEEP_SCENARIO_DIRS=1` for inner `bun test` and prints the temp dir path on completion (read from transcript stub)
`--debug` prints generated test path, MDX→TS source map, resolved `<Variable>` value/display map, and fixture copy map
`--explain` prints MDX→scenario plan without running tests; exits 0
Beta-only flags (`--variant`, `--step`, `--fixture`, `--update-transcript`) fail with `NotImplementedError` citing Phase 3 Beta + `spec/ROADMAP.md`
Tests cover green/red exits, `--explain` snapshot, `--debug` snapshot, unknown-flag rejection
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Implement scenario source-mapper reporter
userStories.description: As a guide author seeing a generated test failure, the reporter rewrites the failure's primary stack frame to point at the MDX line for the failing assertion. (PRD-A2-03 US-068)
acceptanceCriteria: `scripts/test-reporters/scenario-source-mapper.ts` registered as `bun test` reporter for paths matching `test/scenarios/generated/guides/**/*.test.ts`
For each failing assertion, reporter walks stack frames; for frames in generated guide test files, scans backward for nearest `// @source`, `// @scenario`, and always-empty `// @variant` headers
Reporter rewrites primary frame to `<mdx-path>:<line>` and prefixes failure description with `[<guideId>:<scenarioId>] `
Generated `.ts:line` frame preserved as secondary `Generated:` annotation
Frames outside generated guide test files left untouched
`LANDO_DISABLE_GUIDE_SOURCE_MAPPER=1` restores raw `bun test` output unchanged
Fixture pairs under `core/test/scenarios/reporter/`: `(input.txt, expected.txt)` for single-frame failure, multi-frame failure with intervening node-modules frames, and no-match failure
Reporter tolerates always-empty `// @variant:` header without misclassifying as missing
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Print copy-pasteable re-run command on failure
userStories.description: As a guide author looking at a CI failure log, I can copy the last line of the failure block and re-run that exact scenario locally. (PRD-A2-03 US-069)
acceptanceCriteria: On every failed generated guide test, source-mapper reporter appends a single line: `Re-run: bun run docs:scenario <guideId> --scenario <scenarioId>`
Reporter resolves `<guideId>`/`<scenarioId>` from header comments, not file path regex
Missing `@scenario` header falls back to whole-guide form `Re-run: bun run docs:scenario <guideId>` and emits a tagged warning frame
Re-run line is the last line of the failure block (terminals truncating from top still keep it visible)
Tests cover: happy path, missing-`@scenario` fallback, missing-`@source` fallback, and last-line position assertion
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Add per-PR CI gate for guide scenarios
userStories.description: As a maintainer, every PR runs the new generator + type check + scenario tests gate; drift between MDX and generated outputs fails the build. (PRD-A2-04 US-070)
acceptanceCriteria: `scripts/build-ci-workflow.ts` adds `guide-scenarios-linux-x64` job after `static-checks`, in parallel with `provider-integration-linux-x64`
Job runs in order: `bun install` → `bun run codegen` → `bun run typecheck` → `bun run lint:guides` → `bun test test/scenarios/generated/guides/**`
Job is required for merge to `main` per branch protection
Job pinned to `ubuntu-22.04` per ROADMAP cross-cutting Podman-drift risk note
Job uploads any failing scenario's internal transcript JSON (from US-071) as CI artifact `guide-scenario-transcripts-<run-id>.zip` with 7-day retention
`bun run codegen` regenerates `.github/workflows/ci.yml` with the new job; Alpha PRD-07 `git diff --exit-code` drift gate passes
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Persist internal transcripts per scenario run
userStories.description: As a CI consumer, every scenario run produces a deterministic JSON transcript I can attach to a failing PR for debugging. (PRD-A2-04 US-071)
acceptanceCriteria: Transcript stub from US-059 becomes a real write to `dist/transcripts/guides/<guideId>/<scenarioId>.json` at scenario teardown

Effect Schema in `sdk/src/docs/transcript.ts` round-trips through §13.2 schema gate; shape: `{ guideId, scenarioId, render, startedAt, finishedAt, durationMs, exitStatus: "pass"|"fail", frames: TranscriptFrame[] }`

`TranscriptFrame` union: `{ kind: "run", command, stdout, stderr, exit, durationMs }`, `{ kind: "verify", target: "event"|"file"|"errorTag", matched, expected, actual }`, `{ kind: "fixture", name, copiedTo }`, `{ kind: "cleanup", command, exit }`
Apply §19.6 redaction: reuse Alpha PRD-05 `pre-shell-exec` policy; rewrite absolute `testDir` paths to `<testDir>` placeholder; mask timestamps in stdout/stderr
Transcripts land under `dist/transcripts/guides/` (covered by existing `dist/` gitignore rule)

Tests cover: green scenario writes well-formed transcript that schema-validates; red scenario writes transcript with `exitStatus === "fail"` and the failing frame matches the failing assertion; redaction strips a fixture stdout containing each redaction class
Redaction is applied before writing to disk, not on read
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Implement minimal bun run lint:guides
userStories.description: As a maintainer, `bun run lint:guides` checks the Alpha 2 hard rules and fails the build if any guide violates them. (PRD-A2-04 US-072)
acceptanceCriteria: `scripts/lint-guides.ts` entry point, registered as `"lint:guides"` in `package.json#scripts`

Six Alpha 2 rules enforced (each violation exits non-zero with source-mapped error frame): (1) frontmatter validates against `GuideFrontmatter`; (2) every `<Scenario id>` unique within a guide; (3) every `render={false}` scenario declares `reason` ≥ 8 chars; (4) every `<Step name>` unique within its containing scenario; (5) no Beta-only component appears; (6) `diataxis:` (when present) is `tutorial` or `how-to` for guides containing a rendered scenario
Lint runs in US-070 CI gate between `bun run typecheck` and the test run

Rules deferred to Beta documented in `lint-guides.ts` comments with links back to PRD-A2-04: display:execute divergence cap, standalone `<Verify>` matcher-schema deep validation, event-name registry membership, `<Inline>` density caps, `<Hidden>` content shape
Fixture per rule under `core/test/lint/guides/`: one green guide satisfying all rules + one deliberately-broken guide per rule with expected error frame
Rule (6) only fires when frontmatter sets `diataxis:`
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: (Conditional) Implement recipe README strip/flatten
userStories.description: As a recipe author shipping a `recipes/<id>/README.mdx`, the scaffold step copies a prose-only Markdown version into the user's project. Conditional: in scope only if any Alpha recipe ships an MDX README with executable components by PR-open time. (PRD-A2-04 US-073)
acceptanceCriteria: Decision point at PR-open: if no `recipes/*/README.mdx` exists with executable components, close as `Not applicable in Alpha 2; deferred to Beta per §19.13` and update `spec/alpha2/progress.txt`; otherwise implement the bullets below
`scripts/build-recipe-readmes.ts` registered in codegen catalog after `build-guide-scenarios`
For each `recipes/<id>/README.mdx` containing `<Guide>`, writes `recipes/<id>/.scaffold/README.md` (Alpha 2 emits a single prose-only output; per-axis-value cell-product output is Beta+)

Strip rules per §19.13: `<Guide>` unwraps; rendered `<Scenario>` unwraps; `<Step>` becomes numbered heading; `<Run command>` becomes fenced bash block of displayed command; `<Verify>`, `<Hidden>`, test-only scenarios omitted; `<Variable>` replaced by `display` (falls back to `value`); `<Cleanup>` becomes final "Cleanup" section
Recipe scaffold path (Alpha PRD-04 US-031) reads `.scaffold/README.md` if present, falls back to `README.md`

Tests cover fixture recipe with one reader + one hidden scenario produces `.scaffold/README.md` containing only reader steps; no `<` JSX leak; no unresolved `{{variable}}`; corresponding MDX still generates scenario tests via the PRD-A2-02 generator
Decision is made once at PR-open and not re-evaluated mid-series
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
